headline: Kirkwood Pulse • April 25, 2026
overview: Post-game interviews with Cardinals manager Oliver Marmol and pitcher Andre Pallante from the April 24, 2026 game provide local fans with insights into team strategy and player performance, enhancing community engagement with the St. Louis Cardinals. The Mariners narrowly defeated the Cardinals 3-2 at Busch Stadium, marking the Cardinals' first one-run loss this season and highlighting Masyn Winn's RBI streak. This close game impacts the local Cardinals' playoff momentum and sets the stage for Saturday's series continuation with Matthew Liberatore starting. On April 25, local residents can safely dispose of prescription drugs at designated sites including Glendale and Kirkwood police departments and Shrewsbury Walmart from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m., promoting community health and preventing drug misuse. Webster Groves offers ongoing disposal at its police station during business hours.

items.title: Coffee Hour Showcase
items.excerpt: Webster Groves community members age 60 and older are invited to Webster Groves High School’s Frick Theater at Selma and Bradford avenues from 8:30 to 9:30 a.m. on Monday, April 27, for the first Statesmen Coffee Hour Showcase. Doors open…
items.ai_summary: Webster Groves residents aged 60+ are invited to the Statesmen Coffee Hour Showcase at Webster Groves High School on April 27, offering a chance to connect with students and learn about school programs, fostering community engagement across generations.
